---
title: "Multi-Channel Storage Roadmap"
---

## Overview

This roadmap tracks planned improvements to the multi-channel `TensorData`
infrastructure. For documentation of what is already implemented (AnalogToTensor,
TensorToAnalog, TensorCAR, TensorColumnAnalogStorage), see
[Multi-Channel Storage & Cross-Channel Transforms](multi_channel_storage_and_transforms.qmd).

See [GitHub Issue #240](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/240)
for the original feature request.

## Completed

- **toLibTorch() zero-copy stride fix:** `TensorData::toLibTorch()` uses
  `torch::from_blob` with column-major strides, eliminating $O(n)$ virtual calls.
- **AnalogToTensor / TensorToAnalog transforms:** Bridge between independent
  `AnalogTimeSeries` channels and unified `TensorData`. Registered, tested (12 cases).
- **Common Average Reference (CAR) transform:** `TensorData` тЖТ `TensorData`,
  supports mean/median, channel exclusion, CPU/GPU via LibTorch. Registered, tested.
- **TensorColumnAnalogStorage:** Zero-copy `AnalogTimeSeries` views backed by
  `TensorData` columns. `TensorToAnalog` uses this automatically. 17 test cases.
- **ParameterSchema `std::vector<T>` support:** Multi-select lists and CSV line
  edits for vector parameters in `AutoParamWidget`.
- **Phase 4a тАФ Eliminate Armadillo copy overhead (CPU):** Removed `matToFlat()`
  helper from `TensorCAR.cpp`. All three output paths (TimeFrameIndex, Interval,
  Ordinal) now use `std::move(result_mat)` into `arma::fmat` factory overloads,
  reducing copies from 3 to 1. Added `createFromIntervals(arma::fmat)` overload
  to `TensorData`.
- **Phase 4b тАФ LibTorch compute path for TensorCAR:** Added conditional LibTorch
  backend to `TensorCAR` behind `TENSOR_BACKEND_LIBTORCH`. When `use_gpu = true`,
  uses `toLibTorch()` тЖТ device transfer тЖТ `mean`/`median` тЖТ subtract тЖТ download.
  TimeFrameIndex output path is zero-copy via `LibTorchTensorStorage`. TransformsV2
  CMakeLists.txt links Torch privately with `find_package(OpenCV)` workaround for
  the protobuf target conflict.
- **Tensor-backed binary loading (Phase 1 тАФ in-memory path):** Added
  `use_tensor_backed` option to `BinaryAnalogLoaderOptions`. When enabled with
  `num_channels > 1` and in-memory loading, reads interleaved binary тЖТ
  `arma::fmat` тЖТ `TensorData` тЖТ N `TensorColumnAnalogStorage` zero-copy views.
  `BinaryFormatLoader`, `AnalogImport_Widget`, and `BinaryAnalogImport_Widget`
  updated to register both the `TensorData` and per-channel views. Also fixed
  the hardcoded `int16_t` bug in the in-memory multi-channel path тАФ now
  dispatches on `binary_data_type`. 9 test cases (764 assertions).
- **Shared block-cached mmap storage (Phase 2):** Added `SharedMmapBlockCache`
  тАФ a single mmap of an interleaved binary file with a FIFO circular buffer of
  decoded float32 blocks. When one channel loads a time window, all channels
  are decoded. `BlockCachedMmapAnalogStorage` provides per-channel
  `AnalogTimeSeries` views; `MmapTensorStorage` provides `TensorData` access.
  New `TensorData::createTimeSeries2DFromStorage()` factory method for custom
  storage backends. `TensorStorageType::MemoryMapped` added. `loadTensorBacked()`
  updated: `use_tensor_backed + use_memory_mapped + num_channels > 1` takes the
  block-cached mmap path. 5 new test cases (540 assertions).

## Roadmap

### 1. Tensor-Backed Binary Loading {#sec-tensor-loading}

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: Complete (in-memory path).** The mmap path is deferred to Phase 2
(Shared Block Cache).
:::

Today the binary loader
(`src/IO/formats/Binary/analogtimeseries/Analog_Time_Series_Binary.cpp`)
returns independent `AnalogTimeSeries` objects тАФ each channel with either
`VectorAnalogDataStorage` (in-memory) or `MemoryMappedAnalogDataStorage`
(mmap, per-channel stride). No `TensorData` is created and no channels share state.

#### Goals

1. Add `use_tensor_backed` option to `BinaryAnalogLoaderOptions`.
2. When enabled with `num_channels > 1`:
   - **In-memory path:** Read interleaved binary тЖТ `arma::fmat` тЖТ `TensorData`
     тЖТ N `TensorColumnAnalogStorage` views.
   - **Mmap path:** Deferred to shared block cache (see below).
3. Return `BinaryAnalogLoadResult` containing both the `TensorData` (memory
   owner) and per-channel `AnalogTimeSeries` views.
4. Update `AnalogImport_Widget` and `BinaryFormatLoader` to register both the
   `TensorData` and per-channel views in DataManager.
5. Expose `use_tensor_backed` in JSON loading configs (reflect-cpp automatic).
6. Fix the existing hardcoded `int16_t` bug in the in-memory multi-channel path
   (should dispatch on `binary_data_type`).

#### UI Changes

`BinaryAnalogImport_Widget` needs a "Tensor-backed (shared storage)" checkbox,
enabled only when `num_channels > 1`.

#### Files to Modify

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/IO/formats/Binary/analogtimeseries/Analog_Time_Series_Binary.hpp` | Add `use_tensor_backed` option, `BinaryAnalogLoadResult` return type |
| `src/IO/formats/Binary/analogtimeseries/Analog_Time_Series_Binary.cpp` | Tensor-backed in-memory path, data type dispatch fix |
| `src/IO/formats/Binary/BinaryFormatLoader.cpp` | Handle `BinaryAnalogLoadResult` in `loadAnalogBatch()` |
| `src/WhiskerToolbox/DataImport_Widget/AnalogTimeSeries/AnalogImport_Widget.cpp` | Register `TensorData` + per-channel views |
| `src/WhiskerToolbox/DataImport_Widget/AnalogTimeSeries/Binary/BinaryAnalogImport_Widget.cpp` | Add checkbox |

: Files affected by tensor-backed binary loading {.striped}

#### Tests

- Load multi-channel binary with `use_tensor_backed=true`. Verify `TensorData`
  column count matches `num_channels`. Verify each view shares memory
  (pointer comparison). Verify values match legacy independent-channel path.
- Data type dispatch fix: Load float32 binary files with legacy in-memory path.
- JSON round-trip for `BinaryAnalogLoaderOptions` with `use_tensor_backed=true`.

---

### 2. Shared Block-Cached Mmap Storage {#sec-block-cache}

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: Complete.** `SharedMmapBlockCache`, `BlockCachedMmapAnalogStorage`,
and `MmapTensorStorage` are implemented and integrated into `loadTensorBacked()`.
:::

The current per-channel mmap storage (`MemoryMappedAnalogDataStorage`) reads
the same file pages independently per channel. For 384 channels, each OS page
is read ~384 times. Additionally, `getSpanRangeImpl()` always returns an empty
span, forcing element-by-element virtual dispatch with per-element stride
calculation, `memcpy`, type conversion, and scaling.

#### Design: SharedMmapBlockCache

A shared resource that memory-maps an interleaved binary file and maintains a
circular buffer of decoded float blocks.

**New files:**

- `src/DataObjects/AnalogTimeSeries/storage/SharedMmapBlockCache.hpp`
- `src/DataObjects/AnalogTimeSeries/storage/SharedMmapBlockCache.cpp`
- `src/DataObjects/AnalogTimeSeries/storage/BlockCachedMmapAnalogStorage.hpp`
- `src/DataObjects/Tensors/storage/MmapTensorStorage.hpp`

**Key properties:**

- Circular buffer of $N$ decoded blocks (default 16).
- Each block: `block_size_samples ├Ч num_channels` float32 values.
- FIFO eviction (optimized for sequential temporal access).
- Reader-writer lock for thread safety.
- Bulk type conversion at block load time (single vectorized pass via LibTorch
  `from_blob` + `.to(kFloat32)` or plain C++ with SIMD).

**Access pattern:** When DataViewer renders a time window for one channel,
all $N$ channels' data for that window is decoded into the cache. Subsequent
channels hit the cache. The first channel pays the decode cost; the remaining
$N - 1$ are free lookups.

**Cache sizing:**

$$M = N_{\text{blocks}} \times B_{\text{samples}} \times N_{\text{channels}} \times 4 \;\text{bytes}$$

| Channels | Block Size | Num Blocks | Memory |
|----------|-----------|------------|--------|
| 32 | 4096 | 16 | 8 MB |
| 384 | 4096 | 8 | 48 MB |

: Cache memory footprint for common configurations {.striped}

#### BlockCachedMmapAnalogStorage

Per-channel `AnalogTimeSeries` storage backend that delegates to the shared cache:

| Method | Implementation |
|--------|---------------|
| `getValueAtImpl(i)` | O(1) lookup in cached block |
| `getSpanRangeImpl(s, e)` | Span from cache block if within one block; temp buffer for cross-block |
| `isContiguousImpl()` | `false` (interleaved in cache blocks) |

: BlockCachedMmapAnalogStorage CRTP methods {.striped}

#### MmapTensorStorage

`TensorData` storage backend backed by `SharedMmapBlockCache`. Enables loading
interleaved binary files directly as `TensorData` with shape `[time, channels]`.

#### Tests & Benchmarks

- Values match element-by-element against `MemoryMappedAnalogDataStorage`.
- Block loads = тМИrange/block_sizeтМЙ, not range ├Ч num_channels.
- Cross-block read stitching.
- Benchmark: block-cached vs element-by-element for 384-channel data.

---

### 3. Cross-Channel Transforms {#sec-cross-channel-transforms}

Additional transforms that operate on multi-channel `TensorData`:

- **Spatial Whitening (ZCA):** [GitHub Issue #245](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/245)
  тАФ Decorrelates channels and normalizes variances using eigendecomposition.
  Supports ZCA, PCA, and Cholesky methods.

- **Per-Channel IIR Filter:** [GitHub Issue #246](https://github.com/paulmthompson/WhiskerToolbox/issues/246)
  тАФ Applies the same IIR filter to every column of a tensor. Operates on
  contiguous Armadillo columns for optimal cache utilization.

Both follow the `TensorCAR` transform pattern (container transform,
`TensorData` тЖТ `TensorData`, LibTorch compute backend).

---

### 4. GPU-Accelerated Processing {#sec-gpu}

::: {.callout-note}
**Status: Phase 4a and 4b complete.** Ad-hoc benchmarks confirmed large GPU
speedups for CAR transforms. See
[TensorCAR GPU vs CPU Benchmark Analysis](../benchmark/tensor_car_gpu_cpu.qmd)
for full results. Phase 4c (in-place transform support) remains.
:::

#### Benchmark Summary

Ad-hoc benchmarks compared Armadillo CPU, LibTorch CPU, and LibTorch CUDA for
mean-CAR and median-CAR across channel counts (32тАУ384) and block sizes
(1kтАУ900k rows). Key findings at 384 channels:

| Transform | Armadillo (30k rows) | Torch CPU | Torch CUDA | GPU speedup |
|-----------|---------------------|-----------|------------|-------------|
| Mean-CAR  | 11.4 ms             | 7.5 ms    | 0.5 ms     | **15├Ч**     |
| Median-CAR| 126.5 ms            | 32.3 ms   | 2.3 ms     | **14├Ч**     |

- **GPU crossover:** Mean-CAR wins above ~5k rows. Median-CAR wins even at 1k
  rows because the per-row sort is compute-heavy.
- **Threading:** Armadillo is single-threaded for these reductions. LibTorch CPU
  parallelizes across rows using its thread pool (4.2├Ч speedup for median on 6 cores).
  LibTorch CUDA parallelizes most aggressively with one warp per row.
- **Transfer overhead:** CPUтЖФGPU copies saturate PCIe at ~7 Gi/s. At 900k ├Ч 384
  (1.4 GB), transfer takes ~467 ms vs 13 ms compute. GPU wins when data is
  already on-device or the compute exceeds the transfer cost.
- **Recommended `gpu_block_hint`:** 100,000 rows (~3.3 s at 30 kHz, 153 MB at
  384 channels). Achieves 80тАУ95% peak GPU throughput.

#### Production Code Copy Overhead

The current Armadillo-based `TensorCAR` performs 2тАУ3 unnecessary full-matrix
copies per invocation:

1. `arma::fmat result_mat = input_mat` тАФ full copy ($N \times C \times 4$ bytes)
2. `matToFlat(result_mat)` тАФ column-major to row-major conversion (another full copy)
3. `TensorData::createTimeSeries2D(flat)` тАФ copy into new storage (third full copy)

For 384 channels ├Ч 100k rows (153 MB per copy), this wastes ~460 MB of memory
bandwidth. The subtraction itself (`each_col() -= ref`) is in-place on the
first copy тАФ the overhead is entirely in format conversion and storage creation.

#### Implementation Plan

The GPU path decomposes into three sub-phases that can be landed independently.

##### Phase 4a: Eliminate Armadillo Copy Overhead (CPU-only improvement) тЬЕ

::: {.callout-tip}
**Complete.** Removed `matToFlat()` helper. All output paths use
`std::move(result_mat)` into `arma::fmat` factory overloads. Added
`TensorData::createFromIntervals(arma::fmat)`. Copies reduced from 3 to 1.
:::

##### Phase 4b: LibTorch Compute Path for TensorCAR тЬЕ

::: {.callout-tip}
**Complete.** Added `tensorCARLibTorch()` behind `#ifdef TENSOR_BACKEND_LIBTORCH`.
Dispatched when `use_gpu = true`. Uses `toLibTorch()` тЖТ `.to(device)` тЖТ
`mean`/`median` with `index_select` for excluded channels тЖТ subtract тЖТ
`.to(kCPU)` тЖТ `buildResultFromTorch()`. TimeFrameIndex output wraps the result
tensor in `LibTorchTensorStorage` (zero-copy). Interval and Ordinal paths
materialize to flat/arma. TransformsV2 links Torch privately; `find_package(OpenCV)`
before `find_package(Torch)` works around the protobuf target conflict.
:::

##### Phase 4c: In-Place Transform Support

Eliminate the final storage copy by allowing transforms to mutate their input.
This benefits both CPU and GPU paths.

###### Motivation: Container Transform Return-Type Redesign

Today, container transforms return `std::shared_ptr<OutContainer>`, which forces
every invocation to heap-allocate a new data object тАФ even when the transform
could safely mutate the input. `nullptr` is overloaded to mean "failure," so
there is no way for a transform to signal "I mutated the input, reuse it."

The fix changes the container transform signature to return a raw pointer and
accept a non-const reference:

```cpp
// Before (current)
auto tensorCAR(TensorData const& input, TensorCARParams const& params,
               ComputeContext const& ctx) -> std::shared_ptr<TensorData>;

// After
auto tensorCAR(TensorData& input, TensorCARParams const& params,
               ComputeContext const& ctx) -> TensorData*;
```

The raw pointer return encodes three unambiguous states:

| Return value        | Meaning                                         | Executor action                              |
|---------------------|-------------------------------------------------|----------------------------------------------|
| `new TensorData(тАж)` | New allocation (out-of-place)                   | Wrap in `shared_ptr` (takes ownership)       |
| `&input`            | In-place mutation (pointer identity with input) | Reuse original `shared_ptr`                  |
| `nullptr`           | Failure                                         | Throw / propagate error                      |

: Container transform return-value protocol {.striped}

The executor already holds the `shared_ptr` and can perform the address
comparison:

```cpp
auto* raw = transform->execute(*input_ptr, params, ctx);
if (!raw) {
    throw std::runtime_error("transform '" + name + "' failed");
}
if (raw == input_ptr.get()) {
    // In-place: reuse existing shared_ptr, no new allocation
    return DataTypeVariant{input_ptr};
} else {
    // New allocation: take ownership
    return DataTypeVariant{std::shared_ptr<OutContainer>(raw)};
}
```

**Backward compatibility:** Existing transforms that accept `InContainer const&`
bind to `InContainer&` with no source changes. Transforms that currently return
`std::make_shared<Out>(тАж)` need a mechanical change to `new Out(тАж)`.

###### TransformsV2 Plumbing Changes

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `ContainerExecutor.hpp` | `TypedContainerTransform::FuncType`: `InContainer const&` тЖТ `InContainer&`, `shared_ptr<Out>` тЖТ `Out*`. `IContainerExecutor::execute()`: `DataTypeVariant const&` тЖТ `DataTypeVariant&`, return unchanged. |
| `ElementRegistry.hpp` | `registerContainerTransform()`, `executeContainerTransform()`, `RegisterContainerTransform` RAII helper, `TypedContainerExecutor::executeTyped()` тАФ same signature changes. Add pointer-identity check in `TypedContainerExecutor::execute()`. |
| All container transform `.cpp` files | Mechanical: `return std::make_shared<T>(тАж)` тЖТ `return new T(тАж)`; `return nullptr` unchanged. Input parameter `const&` тЖТ `&` (only for transforms that opt into mutation). |

: Files affected by container transform return-type redesign {.striped}

###### TensorCAR In-Place Path

Add `bool in_place = false` to `TensorCARParams`. When true, the transform
mutates the input's storage directly and returns `&input`:

**Armadillo CPU path:**

```cpp
auto* arma_storage = input.storage().getMutableAsChecked<ArmadilloTensorStorage>(
        TensorStorageType::Armadillo);
arma::fmat& mat = arma_storage->mutableMatrix();
mat.each_col() -= ref_col;  // zero output allocation
return &input;
```

**LibTorch CPU path:**

```cpp
auto& t = lt_storage->mutableTensor();
t.sub_(ref);  // PyTorch in-place subtraction
return &input;
```

**LibTorch GPU path:** Upload тЖТ compute тЖТ download тЖТ assign back to
`mutableTensor()`. Eliminates CPU-side output `TensorData` allocation.

**Savings:** Eliminates the remaining storage copy from Phase 4b, bringing
total copy overhead to: 1 upload + 1 download (GPU) or 0 copies (CPU in-place).

#### Temporal Block Processing for Large Datasets

When a dataset exceeds GPU memory, process it in temporal blocks:

1. Iterate over time in chunks of $B$ samples (default $B = 100{,}000$).
2. For each chunk: `toLibTorch()` тЖТ `.to(kCUDA)` тЖТ compute тЖТ `.to(kCPU)` тЖТ
   write back.
3. Concatenate results (or write in-place per block).

This wraps any transform тАФ no per-transform batching code needed.

#### Benchmarks Completed

- тЬЕ GPU vs CPU CAR on 384 channels ├Ч 1kтАУ900k samples.
- тЬЕ Block upload/download overhead measurement (PCIe ~7 Gi/s).
- тЬЕ GPU crossover point determination (mean: ~5k rows, median: always).
- тЬЕ Threading analysis (Armadillo single-threaded, LibTorch multi-threaded).
- тЬЕ In-place vs out-of-place allocation overhead (negligible when original preserved).
- тмЪ GPU vs CPU whitening (requires ┬з3 ZCA implementation).

---

## Dependency Graph

```
Tensor-Backed Binary Loading (┬з1)
  тФЬтФАтФА In-memory path: DONE тЬУ
  тФФтФАтФА Mmap path: DONE тЬУ (uses Shared Block Cache ┬з2)

Shared Block-Cached Mmap (┬з2): DONE тЬУ

Cross-Channel Transforms (┬з3)
  тФЬтФАтФА ZCA Whitening (#245): ready now (uses toLibTorch() тЬУ)
  тФФтФАтФА IIR Filter (#246): ready now (uses Armadillo columns тЬУ)

GPU Acceleration (┬з4)
  тФЬтФАтФА ┬з4a: Eliminate Armadillo copy overhead (no dependencies)
  тФЬтФАтФА ┬з4b: LibTorch compute path (depends on ┬з4a for clean baseline)
  тФФтФАтФА ┬з4c: In-place transform support (independent of ┬з4a/┬з4b, benefits both)
```

## Excluded Scope

- **UI widgets** for multi-channel configuration or channel group management.
- **Spectral transforms** (FFT, spectrogram) тАФ future work using the same
  TensorData infrastructure.
- **`madvise` / `PrefetchVirtualMemory` hints** for mmap prefetching.
- **Distributed / multi-GPU compute.**
